sections.title: Market Pulse: Global Markets Plunge Amid Middle East Escalation

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Drive Broad Sell-Off, Oil and Gold Surge
items.summary: Global stock markets experienced a sharp downturn on Monday, March 2, 2026, as escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, specifically US and Israeli strikes on Iran, triggered a broad risk-off sentiment. Crude oil prices surged dramatically, with Brent crude climbing by as much as 13%, and gold hitting record highs as investors flocked to safe-haven assets. US equity futures pointed to a significantly lower open, reflecting widespread investor caution. Not financial advice.

items.statContext: Signaling a sharply lower open for US markets.
Tech-heavy index poised for significant losses at the open.
Industrial average futures indicating a negative start to trading.

items.headline: Dell Technologies
items.summary: Jumped after strong Q4 results and upbeat FY27 guidance.

items.headline: Block Inc.
items.summary: Gained on a Q4 beat, major cost cuts, and upbeat analyst reactions.

items.headline: Netflix
items.summary: Surged as Netflix exited the Warner Bros. deal, received a significant break fee, and multiple analyst upgrades.

items.headline: CoreWeave
items.summary: Fell after mixed Q4 results and a series of cautious analyst price target cuts.

items.headline: Zscaler
items.summary: Dropped as earnings, guidance, and multiple target cuts failed to support recent expectations.

items.headline: Middle East Conflict Fuels Commodity Rally and Risk Aversion
items.summary: The US and Israeli military campaign against Iran, including coordinated strikes and the death of Iran's Supreme Leader, has dramatically impacted global markets. Fears of supply disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz drove Brent crude oil up to 13% and WTI crude by 7.3% to 9.2%. Gold prices surged to record highs above $5,300 per ounce, as investors sought safe-haven assets amidst the heightened uncertainty. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Defense and Energy Lead, Tech and Airlines Lag Amidst Sector Rotation
items.summary: A clear sector rotation is underway, with investors moving out of higher-risk technology stocks and into more defensive sectors. Defense contractors like Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, and RTX saw gains of 2-3%, while energy giants such as Exxon Mobil and Chevron were up 3-5%. Gold mining stocks also rallied. Conversely, tech stocks (e.g., $NVDA, $AAPL, $MSFT) fell 3-5%, and airlines (e.g., $UAL, $RYAAY, $AAL) dropped 4-8% due to higher fuel costs and travel concerns.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady, Monitors Inflation Outlook
items.summary: The Federal Reserve maintained its benchmark interest rate at 3.5% to 3.75% at its January meeting, and market expectations for a rate cut in March are low. Fed Governor Christopher J. Waller indicated that while January's headline CPI was below expectations, core CPI inflation showed a strong increase. The surge in oil prices due to geopolitical events adds complexity to the inflation outlook and future Federal Reserve policy decisions. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports Provide Company-Specific Insights
items.summary: ADT ($ADT) reported its Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings, showing a 1% and 5% increase in total revenue, respectively. Senior ($SNR) also announced a 20% annual profit rise for H2 2025. This week, investors anticipate Q4 2026 or Q4 2025 earnings from companies including MongoDB ($MDB), EchoStar ($SATS), Credo Technology Group Holding ($CRDO), and Viking Holdings ($VIK), which will offer insights into their financial health and forward guidance.
